name: AZQ Stage 2 Wave A Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 2 Wave A so the Codex runner can evaluate Formam storage foundation patches without hardcoding all task logic.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the patch.

baseline_checks.name: formam_storage_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the new Formam storage module imports cleanly.

STAGE2-WAVEA-01.description: Create Formam package and storage module scaffolding.

checks.name: formam_storage_files_exist
checks.description: Verify the new Formam package and storage module exist.

human_checks: No CLI wiring or command behavior changes were introduced yet.
The patch is limited to Formam scaffolding and tiny import-safe changes only.

STAGE2-WAVEA-02.description: Add canonical Formam path constants and directory helpers.

checks.name: formam_path_helpers_smoke
checks.description: Verify Formam path helpers exist and return stable-looking paths.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['FORM_DIR', 'DATA_FORM_DIR'])
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['DELIVERABLES_DIR', 'FORM_DELIVERABLES_DIR'])
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['MAPS_DIR', 'FORM_MAPS_DIR'])
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['deliverable_file_path', 'get_deliverable_path'])
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['goal_map_path', 'map_file_path', 'get_goal_map_path'])

human_checks: All Formam path ownership now lives in azq/formam/storage.py.
Directory helper behavior is deterministic and readable.

STAGE2-WAVEA-03.description: Define the canonical normalized deliverable schema.

checks.name: deliverable_normalization_schema
checks.description: Verify a partial deliverable record normalizes into the canonical Stage 2 schema.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import storage
partial = {'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Test deliverable'}
fn = getattr(storage, 'normalize_deliverable_record', None) or getattr(storage, 'normalize_deliverable', None)
assert fn is not None, 'normalization helper not found'
result = fn(partial)
for field in ['deliverable_id', 'goal_id', 'title', 'artifact_description', 'dependencies', 'status', 'created']:
    assert field in result, f'missing field: {field}'
assert result['deliverable_id'] == 'DELIV_001'
assert result['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
assert result['title'] == 'Test deliverable'
assert isinstance(result['dependencies'], list)

human_checks: Fallback behavior for missing fields is conservative and explicit.
The normalized schema describes artifact structure, not task work.

STAGE2-WAVEA-04.description: Implement markdown deliverable file serialization and parsing.

checks.name: deliverable_round_trip
checks.description: Verify a canonical deliverable record can round-trip through markdown serialization and parsing.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import storage
record = {
    'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001',
    'goal_id': 'FINIS_001',
    'title': 'Round trip test',
    'artifact_description': 'Produce a visible artifact',
    'dependencies': ['DELIV_000'],
    'status': 'drafted',
    'created': '2026-03-17T00:00:00'
}
serializer = getattr(storage, 'serialize_deliverable_record', None) or getattr(storage, 'deliverable_to_markdown', None)
parser = getattr(storage, 'parse_deliverable_record', None) or getattr(storage, 'deliverable_from_markdown', None)
assert serializer is not None, 'serializer not found'
assert parser is not None, 'parser not found'
text = serializer(record)
parsed = parser(text)
for field in ['deliverable_id', 'goal_id', 'title', 'artifact_description', 'dependencies', 'status', 'created']:
    assert parsed[field] == record[field], f'round-trip mismatch: {field}'

human_checks: The markdown format is human-readable and diff-friendly.
The file format is simple enough for later Agenda code to consume.

STAGE2-WAVEA-05.description: Implement file-backed deliverable repository reads and id allocation.

checks.name: deliverable_repository_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify file-backed read helpers and next-id allocation helpers exist.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['load_deliverable', 'load_deliverable_by_id']), 'missing single-deliverable loader'
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['load_all_deliverables', 'load_deliverables']), 'missing all-deliverables loader'
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['load_deliverables_for_goal', 'load_goal_deliverables']), 'missing goal deliverables loader'
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['next_deliverable_id', 'get_next_deliverable_id']), 'missing next-id helper'

human_checks: Exact lookup uses exact deliverable_id and goal_id matching.

checks.name: goal_validation_helper_exists
checks.description: Verify Formam exposes a dedicated goal validation helper.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['validate_goal_id', 'validate_parent_goal', 'require_goal']), 'missing goal validation helper'

checks.name: goal_validation_checks_exact_goal_id
checks.description: Verify the goal validation helper can be driven with an exact canonical goal id.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.formam import storage
validator = getattr(storage, 'validate_goal_id', None) or getattr(storage, 'validate_parent_goal', None) or getattr(storage, 'require_goal', None)
assert validator is not None
with patch('azq.finis.storage.load_goal', return_value={'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Parent goal', 'status': 'active', 'created': '', 'description': '', 'derived_from': []}):
    result = validator('FINIS_001')
assert result is not None

human_checks: Goal validation is clearly separated from future command-layer presentation.
The helper makes missing-parent failures inspectable rather than silent.
